item.title: Singapore PM Warns of AI Disruptions, Vows to Support Workers
item.description: Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong warned of bigger disruptions ahead as the country navigates a more uncertain global environment, from the Iran conflict to the rapid rise of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Yen Rally Is at Risk of Fading Fast Without More Intervention
item.description: The yen’s intervention-fueled rally is at risk of evaporating quickly, increasing the likelihood that Japan will have to wade back into the market to shore up the exchange rate.

item.title: Record-Low Spreads on EM Asia High-Grade Debt Show Resilience
item.description: Yield premiums on emerging-market Asian investment-grade dollar bonds have tightened to unprecedented levels amid reduced new issuance and China’s economic resilience despite Iran-war disruptions.

item.title: Toto Shares Surge on Plans to Boost Chip Parts Business
item.description: Toto Ltd. shares soared as much as 18%, the most on record, in Tokyo after the toilet maker said it plans to ramp up investment in its chip components business due to strong AI demand.

item.title: Antidepressant Maker Seaport Raises $255 Million in Upsized IPO
item.description: Seaport Therapeutics Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on antidepressants and anxiety drugs, raised nearly $255 million in an upsized US initial public offering that priced at the top of its marketed range.

item.title: OpenAI Finance Chief Sees ‘Vertical Wall of Demand’ for Products
item.description: OpenAI Chief Financial Officer Sarah Friar, rebutting concerns about missing internal targets, said the company is meeting objectives and sees “a vertical wall of demand” for its products.

item.title: Economic Crosscurrents Go Global as Energy Shock Meets AI Wave
item.description: The global economy is increasingly captive to two opposing forces.

item.title: CFTC Reviews Trader Data Report as Kalshi Expands in Commodities
item.description: A top Wall Street regulator is considering changes to a weekly report covering the position of traders just as prediction market exchanges and offshore venues start to offer more contracts in the oldest corner of the derivatives market.

item.title: Japan’s Mimura Declines to Comment on Yen Intervention Talk
item.description: Japan’s top currency official declined to comment on reports that the government intervened in the market to prop up the yen.

item.title: South Korea’s Export Surge Continues as AI Boom Fuels Chip Sales
item.description: South Korea’s exports extended this year’s surge in April as booming semiconductor demand continues to anchor growth, despite mounting risks from higher energy prices linked to the Iran turmoil.

item.title: India’s Heat Exposes a Fragile Grid as Energy Crunch Deepens
item.description: India is likely to endure a blistering summer until monsoon rains arrive in June, with higher-than-average heat already straining power grids at a time when the country is grappling with energy shortages.

item.title: Tokyo Inflation Cools on Childcare Support, Backing BOJ Caution
item.description: Tokyo’s key inflation gauge unexpectedly slowed on the back of expanded subsidies for childcare, staying below the Bank of Japan’s target in a result that supports the bank’s cautious approach toward resuming interest rate hikes.

item.title: Seaport Therapeutics Said to Raise $255 Million in IPO
item.description: Seaport Therapeutics Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on antidepressants and anxiety drugs, raised nearly $255 million in an upsized US initial public offering that priced at the top of its marketed range, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Goldman’s Freund Sees Early Signs of Australia M&A Rebound
item.description: Australia’s subdued deals landscape is showing early signs of recovery after lagging Asia and the US, according to Goldman Sachs’ local head of mergers and acquisitions.

item.title: Novo-Backed Biotech Hemab Raises $301.5 Million in Upsized IPO
item.description: Hemab Therapeutics Holdings Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company that counts Novo Nordisk Foundation as one of its biggest investors, raised $301.5 million in an upsized US initial public offering that priced at the top of a marketed range.

item.title: Petrobras Output Hits Record as Iran War Jolts Oil Markets
item.description: Petrobras pumped record oil and gas volumes in the first quarter and ran its refineries near full capacity, underscoring Brazil’s growing importance to global energy markets disrupted by the Iran war.

item.title: New Zealand Consumer Confidence Hits Three-Year Low on Iran War
item.description: New Zealand consumer confidence has slumped to a three-year low as the Iran war drives up fuel prices, hitting household incomes and stoking fears of an economic slowdown.

item.title: UK Man Agrees to Plead Guilty in ‘Singapore Solution’ Tax Case
item.description: A UK citizen has agreed to plead guilty over his role in helping Americans use offshore accounts to evade taxes through a scheme known as the “Singapore Solution,” US prosecutors said.

item.title: Gold Steadies After Advancing on Japan’s Yen Intervention
item.description: Gold held a gain after the US dollar retreated sharply on Japan’s intervention in the foreign-exchange market.

item.title: US Stock Futures Rise on Tech Earnings, Yen Gains: Markets Wrap
item.description: US equity-index futures opened higher after Wall Street climbed to fresh highs, buoyed by resilient US economic growth and solid corporate earnings, while oil retreated from a four-year peak. The yen gained on a report of Japan’s intervention.

item.title: Warsh Must Confront Risks of Dissenting in His First Fed Vote
item.description: When Kevin Warsh hosts his first meeting as the new chair of the Federal Reserve, he’ll face an immediate dilemma: Stare down White House demands for immediate interest-rate cuts, or stare down fellow policymakers who remain skeptical of the need to ease.

item.title: Oil Holds Weekly Gain as Trump Says Iran Blockade Is Working
item.description: Oil held its second weekly gain as US President Donald Trump said he was sticking with a naval blockade of Iranian ports, elevating concerns the vital Strait of Hormuz would not reopen anytime soon.

item.title: S&P Weighs New Index Rules to Speed Up Addition of Mega IPOs
item.description: S&P Dow Jones Indices LLC has launched a consultation that could eventually speed up the entry of mega cap companies seeking to IPO into its indexes, including the S&P 500.

item.title: First Brands Creditor Sues Auditor BDO Over Missed Red Flags
item.description: A lender that owns First Brands Corp. debt sued auditor BDO USA P.C., alleging it failed to detect problems at the auto parts supplier before it collapsed into bankruptcy and federal prosecutors accused founder Patrick James of fraud.

item.title: How the US Government Plans to Refund Billions in Tariffs
item.description: When the Supreme Court struck down President Trump’s tariffs, it triggered a $166 billion refund process. On today’s Big Take podcast: the logistics of that payout and how soon businesses can expect relief.

item.title: Trader Mercuria Sues Baltic Exchange Over Oil Shipping Benchmark
item.description: Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. is suing the Baltic Exchange over the alleged distortion of a key benchmark for the cost of shipping oil from the Middle East.

item.title: Air Canada Suspends 2026 Guidance Amid Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: Air Canada suspended its full-year 2026 guidance due to uncertainty related to jet fuel prices.

item.title: Venezuela Raises Minimum Wage to $240 Amid Rising Social Unrest
item.description: Venezuela more than doubled the minimum monthly wage to $240 as authorities try to ease pressure on households after months of high inflation and growing unrest.

item.title: Alphabet Has the Second Biggest One-Day Jump in Market Cap Ever
item.description: Alphabet Inc. added a historic amount to its market capitalization on Thursday, following blowout quarterly earnings that sent the stock skyrocketing.

item.title: Tanger Beats Earnings Estimates as Gen Z Drives Return to Physical Stores
item.description: Tanger Inc. posted first-quarter earnings that beat expectations and bumped up its full-year guidance, bolstered by robust tenant occupancy rates and a return to physical retail stores by Gen Z shoppers.
